steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The phrase 'an example of the way in which' demands a choice that shows HOW Ballard blends two traditions in a single work, not one that merely asserts he does so.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Ballard, who wrote *Pan Indian Rhythms* for percussion, is recognized as one of the first composers to blend elements of various Native American musical traditions with Western classical music.: "Ballard, who wrote Pan Indian Rhythms for percussion, is recognized as one of the first composers to blend elements of various Native American musical traditions with Western classical music." This makes a general claim about Ballard's reputation without showing HOW both traditions appear together in a single piece — it asserts blending but does not demonstrate it. ✗
  • Choice B — Blending elements of various Native musical traditions and Western classical music, Ballard composed *Pan Indian Rhythms* for percussion in 1970.: "Blending elements of various Native musical traditions and Western classical music, Ballard composed Pan Indian Rhythms for percussion in 1970." This again asserts blending without identifying a specific element from each tradition that appears in the work together. ✗
  • Choice C — Ballard blended elements of various Native musical traditions with Western classical music in many of his works, including *Pan Indian Rhythms* (1970).: "Ballard blended elements of various Native musical traditions with Western classical music in many of his works, including Pan Indian Rhythms (1970)." This broadens the claim to 'many works' and provides no concrete example of how both elements coexist in a single composition. ✗
  • Choice D — Ballard's composition *Pan Indian Rhythms* is written in a classical form and incorporates Ute notched sticks.: "Ballard's composition Pan Indian Rhythms is written in a classical form and incorporates Ute notched sticks." This names a specific classical element (the form) and a specific Native element (Ute notched sticks) functioning together in one work — a direct example of blending. ✓
D is correct.
